  # Associated bands / musical artists
      assoc_key = "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"
      if assoc_key in pred_map:

      # Background
      if "background" in pred_map:
          clauses.append(f"is a {pred_map['background'][0]}")
  
      # Genre – handle multiple genres
      if "genre" in pred_map:
          genres = fmt_list([g.lower() for g in pred_map["genre"]])
          clauses.append(f"is a {genres} musician")
  
      # Instrument
      if "instrument" in pred_map:
          instruments = fmt_list(pred_map["instrument"])
          clauses.append(f"plays {instruments}")
  
      # Origin
      if "origin" in pred_map:
          clauses.append(f"originates from {pred_map['origin'][0]}")
  
      # Stylistic origin (e.g., Rhythm and blues | stylisticOrigin | Blues)
      if "stylisticOrigin" in pred_map:
          clauses.append(f"has stylistic origin in {pred_map['stylisticOrigin'][0]}")
with:
  # Build a more natural description using key predicates
      # 1. Core musical description (instrument, genre, associated band)
      core_parts = []
  
      # Instrument first (as it often precedes genre)
      if "instrument" in pred_map:
          instruments = fmt_list(pred_map["instrument"])
          core_parts.append(f"plays {instruments}")
  
      # Genre handling – combine with instrument if present
      if "genre" in pred_map:
          genres = fmt_list([g.lower() for g in pred_map["genre"]])
          if core_parts:
              core_parts[-1] += f" {genres}"
          else:
              core_parts.append(f"plays {genres}")
  
      # Associated band / musical artist – link with “with” or “in”
      assoc_key = "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"
      if assoc_key in pred_map:
          bands = fmt_list(pred_map[assoc_key])
          if core_parts:
              core_parts[-1] += f" with {bands}"
          else:
              core_parts.append(f"is associated with {bands}")
  
      # If we built a core phrase, add it as a clause
      if core_parts:
          clauses.append(core_parts[0])
  
  ... (52 more lines)
